message.content:
Repo: /Users/thomasjones/hc-assay (branch harden/pass-2; pass 1 already merged 22 fixes). Venv (run PoCs): /Users/thomasjones/hc-assay/.venv/bin/python.
Dataset-agnostic ML/methodology engine being hardened for PRODUCTION. Hard constraints (NOT bugs): local-first/data-sovereign (loopback only), no metered API (subscription OAuth), engine imports no adapter/dataset (ADR-0002), optional heavy backends lazy-imported (ADR-0006). Read real code; back findings with a concrete repro/PoC. Report ONLY genuine production-readiness defects, not style. Pass-1 findings #101-#122 are already FIXED — do not re-report them; DO report anything they missed or any regression they introduced.

Independent VERIFIER #1 of 2. A pass-2 assessor reported:
TITLE: Frozen StudyDefinition (and peer records) accept a mutable set for frozenset-typed fields, breaking immutability/hashability
DIMENSION: public API & adapter-Protocol stability
SEVERITY: MEDIUM
FILE: src/assay_engine/contracts/study.py:27-51; src/assay_engine/methodology/firewalls.py:90-96 
DESCRIPTION: StudyDefinition is @dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True) and declares modes: frozenset[StudyMode], implying a hashable, immutable value object (it is used as a registry-keyed, pre-registration-anchoring record). But __post_init__ never coerces/validates modes to a frozenset, so passing a plain set (which membership-checks identically, so all internal `StudyMode.X in modes` logic and required_phases(modes) work) is silently accepted. PoC verified: StudyDefinition(..., modes={StudyMode.DISCOVERY}) yields .modes of type set, and hash(definition) then raises `TypeError: unhashable type: 'set'` — a 'frozen' record that is neither hashable nor immutable, and is also mutable in place. The codebase's own test_contract.py:60 constructs StudyDefinition with claims_source=object() relying on direct construction, and the discovery() factory is the only path that coerces to frozenset; the raw constructor (the public API surface a clone uses) does not. DiscoverConfirmSplit (frozenset fields) has the same un-coerced-constructor shape, though from_partition coerces.
SUGGESTED FIX: In StudyDefinition.__post_init__ coerce/validate: `object.__setattr__(self, 'modes', frozenset(self.modes))` (and ideally check each element is a StudyMode). Apply the same frozenset coercion in DiscoverConfirmSplit.__post_init__ so the documented frozen+hashable contract holds regardless of which constructor a clone uses.
Independently determine if this is a TRUE production-readiness problem. Read the code; PoC where applicable. is_real=false if false positive / already handled / documented-justified scope. Recalibrate severity. Give evidence.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	"""Study definition — the adapter's research questions and pre-registration handle.
2	
3	A study definition binds an adapter's pieces together for one concrete run: which parser,
4	which mode, the optional claims source, any extra feature builders, and the research
5	questions. It lives in the instance repository (one repo per study, ADR-0002), not in the
6	engine. The engine reads it to drive the run and to anchor pre-registration.
7	"""
8	
9	from __future__ import annotations
10	
11	from dataclasses import dataclass
12	from enum import Enum
13	from typing import Sequence
14	
15	from assay_engine.contracts.claims import ExternalClaimsSource
16	from assay_engine.contracts.features import FeatureBuilder
17	from assay_engine.contracts.parser import IngestionParser
18	
19	
20	class StudyMode(Enum):
21	    """The two modes from the charter. A study may combine both."""
22	
23	    DISCOVERY = "discovery"
24	    ADJUDICATE_EXTERNAL_CLAIMS = "adjudicate_external_claims"
25	
26	
27	@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
28	class StudyDefinition:
29	    """Everything the engine needs to run one study, supplied by an adapter."""
30	
31	    name: str
32	    modes: frozenset[StudyMode]
33	    parser: IngestionParser
34	    research_questions: tuple[str, ...]
35	    feature_builders: tuple[FeatureBuilder, ...] = ()
36	    claims_source: ExternalClaimsSource | None = None
37	
38	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
39	        if not self.name.strip():
40	            raise ValueError("StudyDefinition.name must be non-empty (it is the registry key)")
41	        if not self.modes:
42	            raise ValueError("StudyDefinition must declare at least one mode")
43	        needs_claims = StudyMode.ADJUDICATE_EXTERNAL_CLAIMS in self.modes
44	        if needs_claims and self.claims_source is None:
45	            raise ValueError("Adjudication mode requires a claims_source; none was provided")
46	        if not needs_claims and self.claims_source is not None:
47	            # A discovery-only study must not carry a claims source — it would risk
48	            # leaking external judgments into a pipeline that is meant to be blind.
49	            raise ValueError(
50	                "claims_source supplied but ADJUDICATE_EXTERNAL_CLAIMS mode not declared"
51	            )
52	
53	    @staticmethod
54	    def discovery(
55	        name: str,
56	        parser: IngestionParser,
57	        research_questions: Sequence[str],
58	        feature_builders: Sequence[FeatureBuilder] = (),
59	    ) -> "StudyDefinition":
60	        return StudyDefinition(
61	            name=name,
62	            modes=frozenset({StudyMode.DISCOVERY}),
63	            parser=parser,
64	            research_questions=tuple(research_questions),
65	            feature_builders=tuple(feature_builders),
66	        )
67

content.text: The finding confirms `__post_init__` does not coerce `modes` to frozenset. Let me look at the firewalls.py reference and DiscoverConfirmSplit, then run a PoC.

content.text: Now let me run the PoC to verify the claims about hashability and the registry usage.

input.command:
cd /Users/thomasjones/hc-assay && .venv/bin/python -c "
from assay_engine.contracts.study import StudyDefinition, StudyMode

class FakeParser:
    pass

# Construct with a plain set instead of frozenset
d = StudyDefinition(
    name='x',
    modes={StudyMode.DISCOVERY},
    parser=FakeParser(),
    research_questions=('q',),
)
print('modes type:', type(d.modes).__name__)
print('membership works:', StudyMode.DISCOVERY in d.modes)
try:
    hash(d)
    print('hash OK')
except TypeError as e:
    print('hash FAILS:', e)

# In-place mutation of the supposedly-frozen record
d.modes.add(StudyMode.ADJUDICATE_EXTERNAL_CLAIMS)
print('after mutation modes:', d.modes)

# Compare with factory
d2 = StudyDefinition.discovery('y', FakeParser(), ['q'])
print('factory modes type:', type(d2.modes).__name__)
try:
    hash(d2)
    print('factory hash OK')
except TypeError as e:
    print('factory hash FAILS:', e)
"

after mutation modes: {<StudyMode.ADJUDICATE_EXTERNAL_CLAIMS: 'adjudicate_external_claims'>, <StudyMode.DISCOVERY: 'discovery'>}
factory modes type: frozenset
factory hash OK

content.content:
src/assay_engine/registry.py:17:StudyFactory = Callable[[], StudyDefinition]
src/assay_engine/contracts/study.py:62:            modes=frozenset({StudyMode.DISCOVERY}),
src/assay_engine/orchestration/gates.py:33:ALL_MODES: frozenset[StudyMode] = frozenset(StudyMode)
---registry---
"""Adapter registration.

A clone registers its :class:`~assay_engine.contracts.study.StudyDefinition` factory under a
name; the engine looks it up to run. This is the only place adapters and the engine meet by
name, and even here the engine holds only a factory callable — it imports no adapter module
itself (ARCHITECTURE.md §3). The instance repository imports the engine, builds its study,
and registers it.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import threading
from typing import Callable, Dict

from assay_engine.contracts.study import StudyDefinition

StudyFactory = Callable[[], StudyDefinition]

_REGISTRY: Dict[str, StudyFactory] = {}
# Registration is normally a one-shot import-time op, but the check-then-act duplicate guard
# is a TOCTOU; a lock makes it atomic if studies are ever registered concurrently (issue #13).
_LOCK = threading.Lock()


def register_study(name: str, factory: StudyFactory) -> None:
    """Register a study factory under ``name``. Raises on duplicate names."""
    with _LOCK:
        if name in _REGISTRY:
            raise ValueError(f"a study named {name!r} is already registered")
        _REGISTRY[name] = factory


def get_study(name: str) -> StudyDefinition:
    """Instantiate the registered study ``name``."""
    try:
        factory = _REGISTRY[name]
    except KeyError:
        raise KeyError(f"no study registered under {name!r}") from None
    return factory()


def registered_studies() -> tuple[str, ...]:
    return tuple(sorted(_REGISTRY))


def clear_registry() -> None:
    """Test helper: drop all registrations."""
    with _LOCK:
        _REGISTRY.clear()

content.text: The registry holds factory callables, not StudyDefinition instances, and StudyDefinition is never actually hashed or stored in a set/dict in production code. Let me verify the pipeline usage where the definition flows through, and check for any equality/dedup that depends on hashing.

feature builders). The callables below are the adapter's *executable* methodology, validated
    against the declared modes: discovery needs ``split``/``discover``/``confirm_held_out``;

    definition).
    """

    definition: StudyDefinition
    source: Path
    baseline_builder: BaselineBuilder
    authority: TimestampAuthority
    split: DiscoverConfirmSplit | None = None

content.text:
This is very telling. Pass-1 finding #113 already established the exact pattern: frozen records that take collection/mapping fields must coerce them in `__post_init__` to be "truly immutable + hashable." The codebase has a `freeze_mapping` helper applied in `claims.py`, `schema.py`, and `pipeline.py`. 

`StudyDefinition.modes` (frozenset field) and `DiscoverConfirmSplit` (frozenset fields) are the conspicuous exceptions that were NOT given the same coercion. This is a genuine consistency/correctness gap that pass-1 #113 missed for these two records.

Let me confirm the DiscoverConfirmSplit raw-constructor PoC and check the `&` operation behavior with a plain set.

input.command:
cd /Users/thomasjones/hc-assay && .venv/bin/python -c "
from assay_engine.methodology.firewalls import DiscoverConfirmSplit

# raw constructor with plain sets
s = DiscoverConfirmSplit({'a','b'}, {'c','d'})
print('discovery_ids type:', type(s.discovery_ids).__name__)
try:
    hash(s)
    print('hash OK')
except TypeError as e:
    print('hash FAILS:', e)
# in-place mutation of frozen record
s.confirm_ids.add('a')
print('after mutation confirm_ids:', sorted(s.confirm_ids))
print('discovery_ids:', sorted(s.discovery_ids))
# does overlap check now lie? a is now in both, but post_init already ran
print('overlap now:', s.discovery_ids & s.confirm_ids)
"

content.content:
discovery_ids type: set
hash FAILS: unhashable type: 'set'
after mutation confirm_ids: ['a', 'c', 'd']
discovery_ids: ['a', 'b']
overlap now: {'a'}

content.text:
Both PoCs confirmed. The DiscoverConfirmSplit case is actually a methodology-correctness issue: its `__post_init__` enforces the Firewall B invariant (disjoint discovery/confirm partitions, non-empty confirm), but with a mutable set the invariant can be silently violated post-construction by in-place mutation — defeating a firewall the whole engine relies on.

Let me check `test_contract.py:60` referenced in the finding to confirm the raw-constructor path is indeed exercised/public.

content.text:
The raw constructor IS the documented public API (exported in `__init__.py`, used directly in tests and reference_study.py with explicit frozenset). The finding is accurate.

I have enough to render a verdict.

Assessment summary:

The finding is **REAL**. Both PoCs reproduce cleanly:
- `StudyDefinition(modes={...})` keeps `.modes` as a plain `set`; the "frozen, slotted" record is then unhashable (`hash()` raises `TypeError`) and mutable in place (`d.modes.add(...)` succeeds).
- `DiscoverConfirmSplit({...}, {...})` via the raw constructor has the same shape, and additionally its Firewall B invariant (disjoint, non-empty partitions) can be silently broken by post-construction in-place mutation — `s.confirm_ids.add('a')` produces a real overlap that `__post_init__` already validated away.

Why it's a genuine production gap and not out-of-scope:
- The codebase already established this exact remediation pattern in pass-1 finding #113 (`pipeline.py:91-92`: "deep-freeze the payload so this frozen record is truly immutable + hashable"), and applies `freeze_mapping` coercion in `contracts/claims.py`, `contracts/schema.py`, and `pipeline.py`. `StudyDefinition.modes` and `DiscoverConfirmSplit` are the two frozen records carrying collection fields that were left un-coerced — an inconsistency/regression-of-omission relative to the project's own immutability contract.
- The raw constructors are public API (both classes are exported from `assay_engine.__init__` / `contracts.__init__` / `methodology.__init__`, and used directly with explicit frozensets in `tests/reference_study.py:120` and `tests/test_pipeline.py:521`). A clone building/cloning these records via the constructor is the realistic path.

Severity recalibration — I downgrade to **LOW** rather than the reported MEDIUM:
- No production code path currently hashes a `StudyDefinition` or stores either record in a set/dict (the registry holds factory *callables*, not instances), so the unhashability does not break any current behavior. The factory (`discovery()`) and `from_partition()` — the idiomatic constructors — already coerce correctly.
- The realistic harm is latent: (a) the documented frozen+hashable contract is false for constructor users, and (b) the DiscoverConfirmSplit firewall invariant is bypassable by in-place mutation. Both are correctness/robustness defects worth fixing, but neither manifests in the current call graph, so impact is low.

The suggested fix is correct and matches the existing #113 pattern: coerce in `__post_init__` via `object.__setattr__(self, "modes", frozenset(self.modes))` (and the same for both fields in `DiscoverConfirmSplit`, before the overlap check), ideally validating element types.

Relevant files:
- `/Users/thomasjones/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/contracts/study.py:32,38-51`
- `/Users/thomasjones/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/methodology/firewalls.py:94-117`
- Precedent: `/Users/thomasjones/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/pipeline.py:91-92`; `/Users/thomasjones/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/_frozen.py`

input.reasoning: VERIFIED REAL but severity downgraded from MEDIUM to LOW. Both PoCs reproduce: StudyDefinition(modes={StudyMode.DISCOVERY}) leaves .modes as a plain set, so hash() raises TypeError (the @dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True) record is neither hashable nor immutable — d.modes.add(...) mutates it in place). DiscoverConfirmSplit raw constructor has the same defect, and additionally its Firewall B invariant (disjoint, non-empty partitions enforced in __post_init__) is silently bypassable by post-construction in-place mutation (s.confirm_ids.add('a') creates a real overlap after validation passed). The raw constructors are genuine public API: both classes are exported from assay_engine.__init__, contracts.__init__, methodology.__init__ and used directly with explicit frozensets in tests/reference_study.py and tests/test_pipeline.py. This is consistent with the codebase's OWN established remediation pattern — pass-1 finding #113 (pipeline.py:91-92 'deep-freeze the payload so this frozen record is truly immutable + hashable') and freeze_mapping coercion in claims.py/schema.py/pipeline.py — meaning study.py and firewalls.py are the two frozen-record exceptions that were left un-coerced, i.e. a real gap pass-1 missed, not out-of-scope. NOT one of the hard constraints (loopback/OAuth/ADR-0002/ADR-0006). Downgraded to LOW because no current production path hashes a StudyDefinition or stores either record in a set/dict (the registry holds factory callables, not instances), and the idiomatic constructors discovery()/from_partition() already coerce correctly — so the harm is latent (false documented contract + a mutation-bypassable firewall invariant) rather than an active failure. The suggested fix is correct and matches the #113 pattern.
input.evidence: PoC 1 (study.py): StudyDefinition(name='x', modes={StudyMode.DISCOVERY}, parser=FakeParser(), research_questions=('q',)) -> modes type: set; membership works: True; hash FAILS: unhashable type: 'set'; after .modes.add(...) modes contains both enum members (frozen record mutated in place). Factory StudyDefinition.discovery(...) -> modes type: frozenset; hash OK. PoC 2 (firewalls.py): DiscoverConfirmSplit({'a','b'}, {'c','d'}) -> discovery_ids type: set; hash FAILS: unhashable type: 'set'; after s.confirm_ids.add('a') -> overlap now: {'a'} (Firewall B disjointness invariant violated post-construction, after __post_init__ already validated no overlap). Code: contracts/study.py:27-51 __post_init__ never coerces self.modes; firewalls.py:90-117 __post_init__ does the overlap/non-empty check on raw fields without frozenset coercion (only from_partition coerces). Precedent for fix already in repo: pipeline.py:91-92 and src/assay_engine/_frozen.py freeze()/freeze_mapping().

content.text: Verdict recorded. The finding is REAL but I recalibrated severity from MEDIUM to LOW — no current production path hashes these records or relies on their immutability (registry stores factory callables, not instances; idiomatic constructors already coerce), so the defect is latent rather than active. It remains a genuine production-readiness gap because it contradicts the codebase's own immutability contract (established in pass-1 #113 and applied via `freeze_mapping` elsewhere), and because the `DiscoverConfirmSplit` Firewall B disjointness invariant is bypassable by in-place mutation. The two frozen records carrying collection fields — `StudyDefinition.modes` and `DiscoverConfirmSplit` — are the ones that were left un-coerced, and the suggested fix matches the existing pattern.
